name: arXiv MCP Server
what_it_does: MCP server that enables AI assistants to search, download, and analyze academic papers from arXiv, with local caching and specialized research analysis prompts.
use_cases: Searching arXiv for papers by topic, date range, or category
Downloading and reading academic papers through AI assistants
Literature review workflows with structured analysis prompts
Keeping up with latest research in specific fields
Building local paper libraries for offline access
not_for: Accessing papers behind paywalls (arXiv is open-access only)
Non-academic content or general web search
best_when: You need AI assistants to search, retrieve, and analyze academic papers from arXiv as part of a research workflow.
avoid_when: You need access to papers beyond arXiv (e.g., behind paywalls), or you need full citation management capabilities.

auth.notes: No authentication required. arXiv API is publicly accessible. No API key needed.

pricing.notes: Apache-2.0 licensed. Fully free. arXiv API is also free and public.

performance.rate_limits: Subject to arXiv API rate limits (typically 1 request per 3 seconds)

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: No error handling patterns documented in README. 4 tools with clear purposes but no documented error responses or failure modes. Small codebase (84 commits) suggests basic error handling.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Search and read operations are inherently idempotent. Downloads cache locally, so repeated downloads are safe.
known_agent_gotchas: Subject to arXiv API rate limits - rapid successive searches may be throttled
Paper storage path defaults to ~/.arxiv-mcp-server/papers - can accumulate disk space
Requires Python 3.11+ which may not be available on all systems
No documented pagination for search results - unclear how many results are returned per query
deep-paper-analysis prompt is a nice feature but quality depends on the AI model used
No documented handling of arXiv API downtime or errors
